title: Timothée Chalamet Wins Golden Globe for Marty Supreme, Says Previous Awards Losses Make This Moment That Much Sweeter
text: Timothée Chalamet won the Golden Globe for Best Performance By a Male Actor in a Motion Picture — Musical or Comedy for his role in Marty Supreme, where he played a table tennis champion. During his acceptance speech, Chalamet expressed that his previous losses at the Golden Globes made this victory especially meaningful. He stated: those moments didn't make this moment that much sweeter while acknowledging past nominations for films including Wonka, Beautiful Boy, and Call Me By Your Name. The actor thanked director Josh Safdie, writer Ronald Bronstein, and his co-stars, along with expressing gratitude to his parents and partner Kylie Jenner. He humorously referenced thanking Kevin O'Leary (Mr. Wonderful from Shark Tank), noting he would have been stunned if told this would happen at age 19.
